---
title: "Baldwin's Nutrush (Scleria baldwinii)"
slug: "scleria-baldwinii"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/257755679/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Baldwin's Nutrush"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Liliopsida"
tax_order: "Poales"
tax_family: "Cyperaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Liliopsida", "Poales", "Cyperaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/257755679/medium.jpg" alt="Baldwin's Nutrush" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/alexthedryad">
alexthedryad</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/149569889">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Liliopsida
-   **Order:** Poales
-   **Family:** Cyperaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=168652)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Rare species in Florida found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and seepage areas.

## Natural history notes

A grass-like wetland plant characterized by its slender stems, narrow leaves, and small, hard, nut-like fruits typical of nutrushes. In Florida, it is found in moist to wet habitats such as flatwoods, bogs, and seepage areas, often in sandy soils. It grows during the warmer months and contributes to plant diversity and structure in wetland communities.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/168652-Scleria-baldwinii)
-   [Local profile](https://florida.plantatlas.usf.edu/plant/species/2686)
